configEntries.description: Python executable used to run pip for user-site packages.

scripts.name: Check pip User Updates
scripts.description: Checks pip --user packages and emits stable dashboard parser lines.

steps.label: Check pip user updates
steps.command:
py="{{config.pythonCommand}}"
[ -n "$py" ] || py=python3
"$py" -m pip list --user --outdated --format=json 2>/dev/null | "$py" -c 'import json,sys
for p in json.load(sys.stdin):
 print("LUDASH_UPDATE %s %s %s" % (p["name"], p["version"], p["latest_version"]))'

scripts.name: Detect pip User
scripts.description: Checks whether Python and pip are available.

steps.label: Detect pip user
steps.command:
py="{{config.pythonCommand}}"
[ -n "$py" ] || py=python3
command -v "$py" >/dev/null 2>&1 && "$py" -m pip --version >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo found

scripts.name: List Installed pip User Packages
scripts.description: Reports pip --user packages for package inventory.

steps.label: List pip user packages
steps.command:
py="{{config.pythonCommand}}"
[ -n "$py" ] || py=python3
"$py" -m pip list --user --format=json 2>/dev/null | "$py" -c 'import json,sys
for p in json.load(sys.stdin):
 print("LUDASH_PACKAGE %s %s" % (p["name"], p["version"]))'

steps.label: Update pip user packages
steps.command:
py="{{config.pythonCommand}}"
[ -n "$py" ] || py=python3
out="$("$py" -m pip list --user --outdated --format=json 2>/dev/null)"
pkgs="$(printf '%s' "$out" | "$py" -c 'import json,sys; print(" ".join(p["name"] for p in json.load(sys.stdin)))')"
[ -z "$pkgs" ] || "$py" -m pip install --user --upgrade $pkgs
